results.why: These swaps tighten [[Atraxa, Praetors' Voice]] around efficient tutoring, stronger counters synergy, and cleaner high-impact interaction. The pass also trims redundant X-draw spells and excess basic lands to improve card quality at a casual power level. [[Farewell]] and [[Teferi's Protection]] add reliable reset and protection tools, while [[Vorinclex, Monstrous Raider]] and [[Ichormoon Gauntlet]] better support the deck’s proliferate game plan.

The pass also trims redundant X-draw spells and excess basic lands to improve card quality at a casual power level. added.demonic tutor: [[Demonic Tutor]] upgrades consistency by finding the deck’s best card for any board state.
added.drown in dreams: [[Drown in Dreams]] scales well with big mana and can convert proliferate support into flexible card draw or pressure.
added.farewell: [[Farewell]] gives the deck a clean, flexible sweeper that answers multiple permanent types at once.
added.ichormoon gauntlet: [[Ichormoon Gauntlet]] turns planeswalkers into stronger proliferate engines and fits the counters plan well.
added.teferi's protection: [[Teferi's Protection]] protects your board and life total from wipes or swing turns efficiently.
added.vorinclex, monstrous raider: [[Vorinclex, Monstrous Raider]] dramatically increases counter output while slowing opposing counters strategies.

removed.swamp: A second [[Swamp]] was cut because the deck benefits more from higher-impact spells than extra basics here.
removed.blue sun's zenith: [[Blue Sun's Zenith]] was removed as a less efficient draw sink than the new options.
removed.fact or fiction: [[Fact or Fiction]] was cut to favor more decisive effects over medium-value card selection.
removed.nicol bolas, dragon-god: [[Nicol Bolas, Dragon-God]] falls outside [[Atraxa, Praetors' Voice]] color identity and had to be removed.
removed.pull from tomorrow: [[Pull from Tomorrow]] was trimmed as another redundant X-draw spell in the slot upgrade.
removed.stroke of genius: [[Stroke of Genius]] was removed to reduce overlap among expensive instant-speed draw finishers.

results.summary: Transformed: Curve tightening. Power Casual, budget Moderate. Avg CMC ~2.84.
results.why: This pass lowers the deck’s average mana value by replacing several expensive, slower cards with cheaper interaction, ramp, and proliferate support for [[Atraxa, Praetors' Voice]]. The added lands and fast mana improve early fixing, while low-cost draw and counters keep turns efficient. It also trims clunky top-end threats that do not advance the tighten-curve plan well. One off-color card was also removed to respect [[Atraxa, Praetors' Voice]]'s color identity.

raw.plan: This pass shifts the deck toward the stated interaction goal by replacing slower utility lands and big card-draw spells with efficient answers. It also cleans up the list by cutting [[Nicol Bolas, Dragon-God]], which sits outside the commander's color identity.

results.why: This pass lowers the deck’s average mana cost so [[Ghired, Conclave Exile]] can develop pressure earlier and keep producing value without waiting on expensive top-end. To make room, the cuts trim excess lands and a few expensive, slower finishers that were less important to the interaction plan.
raw.why: This pass adds efficient, flexible interaction so [[Ghired, Conclave Exile]] can answer problematic permanents instead of only advancing its own board. The new package improves coverage against artifacts, enchantments, creatures, and tokens while keeping the deck in a casual range. To make room, the cuts trim excess lands and a few expensive, slower finishers that were less important to the interaction plan.

added.aura shards: [[Aura Shards]] turns your creature and token production into repeatable artifact and enchantment removal.
added.beast within: [[Beast Within]] is a flexible answer to nearly any permanent at instant speed.
added.chaos warp: [[Chaos Warp]] gives Naya a clean way to answer troublesome permanents of any type.
added.cinder vines: [[Cinder Vines]] adds cheap interaction for artifacts and enchantments with incidental pressure.
added.hull breach: [[Hull Breach]] is an efficient two-mana answer that can tag two targets.
added.krosan grip: [[Krosan Grip]] removes key artifacts or enchantments through split second.
added.reclamation sage: [[Reclamation Sage]] adds a creature-based removal piece that fits a token deck well.
added.return to dust: [[Return to Dust]] provides strong exile-based artifact and enchantment removal.
added.stroke of midnight: [[Stroke of Midnight]] is a versatile instant that answers any nonland permanent.
added.wear // tear: [[Wear // Tear]] is cheap, flexible interaction with the option to hit two targets.
added.winds of abandon: [[Winds of Abandon]] gives the deck scalable creature interaction and a reset button.

removed.mountain: A second [[Mountain]] was cut to make room while keeping the mana base functional.
removed.plains: A second [[Plains]] was trimmed to balance the higher spell count.
removed.giant adephage: [[Giant Adephage]] is a slower top-end threat that contributes less to interaction.
removed.godsire: [[Godsire]] is powerful but very expensive and was cut for lower-cost answers.
removed.grove of the guardian: [[Grove of the Guardian]] is a slower utility land that was less important than cleaner interaction.
removed.rogue's passage: [[Rogue's Passage]] was trimmed because utility evasion mattered less than adding answers.
removed.utvara hellkite: [[Utvara Hellkite]] is a costly finisher that was less necessary after improving interaction.
removed.worldspine wurm: [[Worldspine Wurm]] is a high-end threat that was cut to lower clunkiness and add answers.

results.why: This pass lowers the deck’s intensity and makes its play pattern more casual by trimming a premium protection piece and some less-essential slots for broader token payoffs and smoother setup. [[Boros Signet]], [[Fellwar Stone]], [[Exotic Orchard]], and [[Krosan Verge]] help the mana come together more naturally, while [[Beastmaster Ascension]], [[Emmara Tandris]], and [[Song of the Worldsoul]] lean into big board-centric token gameplay. The result keeps [[Ghired, Conclave Exile]] focused on combat and token fun without pushing toward higher-power finishes.

raw.plan: This pass tones down [[Yuriko, the Tiger's Shadow]] by cutting extreme free interaction, top-end haymakers, and premium tutors in favor of simpler ramp, fair removal, and combat-based card draw. The added cards support a slower, more interactive ninja plan without spiking the deck's ceiling. The mana base also shifts slightly toward blue to better support cheap evasive creatures and ninjutsu lines.
raw.why: This pass tones down [[Yuriko, the Tiger's Shadow]] by cutting extreme free interaction, top-end haymakers, and premium tutors in favor of simpler ramp, fair removal, and combat-based card draw. The added cards support a slower, more interactive ninja plan without spiking the deck's ceiling. The mana base also shifts slightly toward blue to better support cheap evasive creatures and ninjutsu lines.

added.island: Adding [[Island]] improves blue consistency for early evasive plays and ninjutsu support.
added.access tunnel: [[Access Tunnel]] helps small creatures connect so ninjas can keep triggering.
added.bident of thassa: [[Bident of Thassa]] rewards attacking with steady card draw in a fair way.
added.curtains' call: [[Curtains' Call]] is solid multiplayer removal that fits a slower casual pace.
added.feed the swarm: [[Feed the Swarm]] gives the deck a clean answer to enchantments and creatures.
added.go for the throat: [[Go for the Throat]] is straightforward creature removal at an efficient rate.
added.moon-circuit hacker: [[Moon-Circuit Hacker]] is a low-pressure ninja that adds card flow through combat.
added.path of ancestry: [[Path of Ancestry]] adds fixing while supporting the deck's shared creature type.
added.prosperous pirate: [[Prosperous Pirate]] is a slower value creature that helps ramp without being explosive.
added.prosperous thief: [[Prosperous Thief]] makes attacking ninjas generate mana in a fair, thematic way.
added.reality shift: [[Reality Shift]] is flexible removal that answers problematic creatures cleanly.
added.reconnaissance mission: [[Reconnaissance Mission]] turns combat damage into steady cards for the whole attack plan.
added.serum visions: [[Serum Visions]] offers simple card selection without the efficiency of top-tier tutors.
added.thousand-faced shadow: [[Thousand-Faced Shadow]] is an evasive one-drop that supports casual ninjutsu sequencing.
added.wayfarer's bauble: [[Wayfarer's Bauble]] provides basic ramp that is useful but appropriately modest for casual games.

removed.swamp: Cutting [[Swamp]] makes room for more blue sources and utility lands.
removed.blinkmoth infusion: [[Blinkmoth Infusion]] is an expensive payoff that is too clunky for this casual build.
removed.cloud of faeries: [[Cloud of Faeries]] is trimmed to reduce efficiency and free-value play patterns.
removed.consult the necrosages: [[Consult the Necrosages]] is removed for more reliable and streamlined options.
removed.cyclonic rift: [[Cyclonic Rift]] is a high-impact staple that pushes games too hard for the target power.
removed.dismember: [[Dismember]] is replaced with removal that is less rate-pushed and less punishing.
removed.enter the infinite: [[Enter the Infinite]] is an extreme top-end card that does not fit a relaxed game plan.
removed.fabricate: [[Fabricate]] is cut to lower tutor density and make games play out more organically.
removed.force of will: [[Force of Will]] is removed to soften the deck's free interaction and overall sharpness.
removed.mystical tutor: [[Mystical Tutor]] is cut to reduce consistency and repetitive setup lines.
removed.ninja of the new moon: [[Ninja of the New Moon]] is a low-efficiency body that is outclassed by cleaner role-players.
removed.temporal trespass: [[Temporal Trespass]] can create swingy extra-turn sequences that are less casual-friendly.
removed.vampiric tutor: [[Vampiric Tutor]] is removed to lower the deck's precision and power ceiling.

Overall, the deck should play a little slower and more board-focused while still supporting the Elf game plan.

added.blighted woodland: [[Blighted Woodland]] adds slower, fair ramp from a land slot.
added.canopy tactician: [[Canopy Tactician]] is a simple tribal lord that supports combat without spiking power too hard.
added.casualties of war: [[Casualties of War]] gives flexible, high-cost interaction that suits a slower casual table.
added.dwynen, gilt-leaf daen: [[Dwynen, Gilt-Leaf Daen]] rewards going wide with Elves in a combat-focused way.
added.elderfang venom: [[Elderfang Venom]] adds flavorful tribal payoff and deathtouch pressure instead of burst power.
added.elvish warmaster: [[Elvish Warmaster]] steadily builds an Elf board without ending games as abruptly as top-end finishers.
added.golgari rot farm: [[Golgari Rot Farm]] trades speed for casual mana fixing.
added.jungle hollow: [[Jungle Hollow]] is a slower dual land that fits a relaxed manabase.
added.leaf-crowned visionary: [[Leaf-Crowned Visionary]] adds fair tribal card flow and another Elf lord effect.
added.miara, thorn of the glade: [[Miara, Thorn of the Glade]] gives incremental card draw when your Elves die.
added.myriad landscape: [[Myriad Landscape]] provides slower land-based ramp that supports a lower-powered build.
added.oran-rief, the vastwood: [[Oran-Rief, the Vastwood]] offers modest board growth from a tapped utility land.
added.pact of the serpent: [[Pact of the Serpent]] is a tribal draw spell that keeps the deck refilling in a fair way.
added.poison-tip archer: [[Poison-Tip Archer]] turns creature trades into gradual pressure rather than a sudden combo finish.
added.prowess of the fair: [[Prowess of the Fair]] gives the deck resilient, thematic value when Elves die.
added.realmwalker: [[Realmwalker]] adds steady tribal card advantage tied directly to your creature type.
added.skemfar shadowsage: [[Skemfar Shadowsage]] provides a scaled Elf payoff that is strong but not overly explosive.
added.skullclamp: [[Skullclamp]] converts small creatures into card draw and supports grindier games.
added.tainted wood: [[Tainted Wood]] improves color fixing at a more casual pace than premium lands.
added.temple of malady: [[Temple of Malady]] smooths draws with slower fixing that matches the pass goal.
added.tyvar kell: [[Tyvar Kell]] is a tribal planeswalker payoff that supports Elf boards over multiple turns.
added.wolverine riders: [[Wolverine Riders]] adds slow, repeatable token production and life gain for longer games.

removed.forest: Extra [[Forest]] copies were cut to make room for more utility and dual lands.
removed.swamp: Extra [[Swamp]] copies were cut to make room for more utility and dual lands.
removed.allosaurus shepherd: [[Allosaurus Shepherd]] is a premium Elf staple that pushes efficiency and closing power too high.
removed.beast whisperer: [[Beast Whisperer]] was trimmed for a less optimized draw engine package.
removed.craterhoof behemoth: [[Craterhoof Behemoth]] is a classic sudden finisher that is too explosive for a more casual pass.
removed.damnation: [[Damnation]] was removed in favor of less swingy, more selective interaction.
removed.deadly rollick: [[Deadly Rollick]] is extremely efficient removal that raises the deck's overall power.
removed.deathrite shaman: [[Deathrite Shaman]] is a highly efficient staple that is stronger than this pass wants.
removed.ezuri, claw of progress: [[Ezuri, Claw of Progress]] is off-plan for [[Lathril, Blade of the Elves]] and not needed here.
removed.finale of devastation: [[Finale of Devastation]] is both tutor and finisher, making games end too efficiently.
removed.gaea's cradle: [[Gaea's Cradle]] generates explosive mana that is well above a casual manabase.
removed.selvala, heart of the wilds: [[Selvala, Heart of the Wilds]] is a powerful engine that accelerates the deck too much.
removed.shared animosity: [[Shared Animosity]] makes wide boards lethal very quickly and increases burst damage.
removed.triumph of the hordes: [[Triumph of the Hordes]] is an abrupt infect finisher that pushes games to end suddenly.
removed.urza's incubator: [[Urza's Incubator]] is a strong tribal cost reducer that speeds the deck up too much.
